calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: MECH-204 step-size sweep at extended cycle count (16 cycles vs 541b's 4)
items.note: Companion to V3-EXQ-541b (NOT a supersession). 541b PASSed C1+C2+C3 in every step-size arm but FAILed C4 (cross-arm divergence) with a clean monotone dose-response curve: step 0.05 -> 0.31%, 0.10 -> 0.63%, 0.25 -> 1.56%, 0.50 -> 3.13%. None reached 5% threshold. 541c uses sleep_loop_K=1 + EPISODES_PER_RUN=16 to give 16 cycles per run (4x 541b's 4 cycles), same 5 step arms, same env, same seeds. Tests whether F1 cross-arm divergence scales linearly with cycle count (F1 sufficient given enough exposure -> Phase 7 stays deferred per REM-precision lit-pull SYNTHESIS dispatch case #1) OR plateaus at 4-cycle level (F1 at its ceiling -> Phase 7 / Option B becomes load-bearing per dispatch case #3). Lit-pull SYNTHESIS (5 entries, MECH-204 lit_conf 0.864) has Hobson-Hong-Friston 2014 + Walker-Stickgold 2006 supporting F1-sufficient and Laukkonen-Friston-Chandaria 2025 hyper-model proposal supporting dual-arm F3. validate_experiments + dry-run smoke both OK. Auto-claim by Mac runner expected within seconds.
